works.title: Dream Compendium No. 2
works.description: Antiphonal counterpoint for two saxophones and two harpsichords, built from tight hocketing and stereo call-and-response.

works.title: Heterophony with Birds
works.description: Slow canon/heterophony for plucked strings with bird field-recording layer; expands into high-register synth lines late in the form.

works.title: Boss Pattern (7+9)
works.description: High-energy synth sketch in 16/8 (7+9), driven by a continuous bass line and interlocking lead figures designed for combat pacing.

works.title: Arpeggio Scaffold
works.description: Voice-led theme over measured organ arpeggiation; a harmonic frame intended for later orchestration and contrapuntal extension.

works.title: Three-Voice Lament
works.description: Short three-voice plucked sketch centred on lament contour and clear contrapuntal spacing.

works.title: Dirge in Three
works.description: Slow processional polyphony with sparse drum pulse; layered voices carry the texture.

works.title: Chant with Bells
works.description: Chant-like vocal texture with bell tones outlining the melodic skeleton and echoing below with resonant decay.

works.title: Guillaume de Machaut — Dame, ne regardes pas
works.description: Two-voice Ars Nova excerpt, sung as layered untexted vocals in a low register; sustained resonance emphasises weight and intervallic friction.

works.title: Nocturne Reed
works.description: Monophonic lead inspired by French Romantic organ reeds, with key-tracked wavetable colour and fast portamento for expressive articulation.

works.title: VEDGALM
works.description: Growling bass derived from a monster-truck engine recording, shaped into a wavetable and driven with saturation; a compact sound design study.

works.title: Adam de la Halle — Tant con je vivrai
works.description: Polyphonic excerpt sung in a low register with deliberate electronic processing applied as a stylistic layer.

works.title: Morales — Pie Jesu (from Missa pro defunctis)
works.description: Multitracked solo voices forming a small Renaissance choral texture; tuned for blend and cadential weight.

works.title: Faenza Codex — Constantia
works.description: Two-part Faenza Codex piece performed on kantele, with clean articulation and clear line separation.

works.title: Schein — Sonata Padouana
works.description: Excerpt from Schein’s Banchetto musicale rendered vocally; a restrained tactus outlines the slow dance form.

works.title: Shostakovich — Symphony No. 11 (excerpt)
works.description: Orchestral mock-up of a climactic passage from Symphony No. 11, built using modern sample libraries with articulated dynamics and percussion weight.

works.title: Philippus de Caserta — De ma dolour
works.description: Soft multitracked vocal rendering of an Ars Subtilior chanson, focused on blend and fragile contour.

works.title: Hasapiko — Polyphonic Harmonisation
works.description: Traditional Greek melody reharmonised in a late-medieval contrapuntal style, moving from source to extracted tune to full polyphonic realisation.

works.title: Preston — Uppon La Mi Re
works.description: Keyboard performance built on a repeating hexachord ground, with contrapuntal elaboration above the bass.
